title: RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 record + lease acquisition primitive
goal: Implement the durable RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 record (Postgres) and the lease acquisition + extension + release primitive that keeps exactly one active executor on any exclusive or handoff-style mailbox thread. Closes the folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-Executor-Routing-Claim-Lease-v1 stub's 'one lawful ownership layer' requirement.
expected_diff_shape: New Postgres migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/NNN_role_mailbox_claim_lease.sql; new Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/lease.rs with `LeaseManager::acquire / extend / release / takeover` methods + `LeaseError` enum; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_lease_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-177 repo + Postgres infrastructure available so the lease table can FK to role_mailbox_thread.
implementation_notes: Per spec v02.186 §02-system-architecture.md role mailbox subsection [ADD v02.176] lines 6151-6156: define `RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 { lease_id: Uuid v7, thread_id: Uuid v7 (FK), holder_executor_kind: ExecutorKind, holder_role_id: RoleId, holder_session_id: Uuid v7, acquired_at_utc, expires_at_utc, released_at_utc: Option, takeover_of: Option<Uuid v7> (FK to predecessor lease), takeover_reason: Option<String> }`. Postgres migration creates the table with a partial unique index `WHERE released_at_utc IS NULL AND expires_at_utc > now()` keyed by thread_id — this enforces 'one active unexpired lease per thread' at the database level per spec line 6156. `LeaseManager::acquire(thread_id, requesting: LeaseRequest) -> Result<RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1, LeaseError>` runs inside a transaction: (1) SELECT thread FOR UPDATE; (2) check thread.claim_mode allows requesting.executor_kind via executor_kind_allowlist; (3) check no active lease (or, if claim_mode=Handoff, allow takeover per takeover_policy); (4) INSERT lease row. LeaseError variants: `LeaseHeldByOther { current_holder }`, `ExecutorKindNotAllowed`, `ThreadInTerminalState`, `Conflict`. `extend(lease_id, extra_secs)` is idempotent and only succeeds if lease is still unexpired and unreleased. `release(lease_id)` sets released_at_utc and is a no-op if already released. `takeover(thread_id, requesting, predecessor_lease_id, reason)` permitted only when thread.takeover_policy allows (TakeoverPolicy enum: Never|OnLeaseExpiry|AlwaysWithReason|OperatorOnly); writes new lease row with takeover_of and takeover_reason set, and force-releases the predecessor with reason recorded. Tests (use Postgres test container): (a) acquire on fresh thread succeeds; (b) second acquire returns LeaseHeldByOther; (c) acquire after lease expiry succeeds; (d) takeover with Never policy returns error; (e) parallel acquire from 8 tasks — exactly one wins; (f) extend after expiry returns error; (g) audit row chain is queryable via lease_id ancestry.
risk_if_missed: Without a lease primitive, the cluster X preservation slice's central invariant (one active executor per exclusive thread, no races between local + cloud models + reviewers + validators + operators + automation) is silently violated. The folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-Executor-Routing-Claim-Lease-v1 intent is lost.
validator_focus: Partial unique index prevents two active leases on the same thread at the database level (not application level); parallel-acquire test asserts exactly-one-winner; takeover policy enforced atomically with predecessor release.

minimum_controls: Database-level partial unique index proven by an INSERT-conflict test bypassing the LeaseManager — must fail with a Postgres unique-violation error.
Takeover audit chain queryable via recursive CTE so an auditor can reconstruct the full ownership history of any thread.
Lease extension cannot bypass expiry (extend after expires_at_utc returns LeaseExpired).
